title: Good Friday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
April 2, 2021 | Good Friday Sermon by Katie Pearson

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

Good Friday 2021 fell on April 2, the culmination of Holy Week in the Christian calendar during Lent. The Revised Common Lectionary readings for Good Friday would have included Isaiah 52:13-53:12 (the Suffering Servant), Psalm 22, Hebrews 10:16-25, and John 18:1-19:42 (the Passion narrative). This is the most solemn day of the church year, emphasizing Christ's crucifixion and redemptive sacrifice, typically observed with stripped altars, no Gloria, and mournful liturgy.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church in 2021 was navigating post-pandemic reopening protocols, with many dioceses still managing COVID-19 restrictions on in-person worship. The Diocese of Colorado (which includes Saint John's Cathedral in Denver) was in the midst of its regular operations under Bishop Robert Wright, though specific diocesan news from this exact week is not readily confirmed. The broader church continued conversations around racial justice and inclusion that had intensified following 2020's racial reckoning.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing early spring weather in the first week of April 2021, with typical Colorado April variability. Colorado was advancing its COVID-19 vaccination campaign, with eligibility expanding during this period as the state moved through Phase 1B. The Denver metro area and Capitol Hill neighborhood were gradually reopening businesses and cultural venues as vaccination rates increased, though restrictions remained in place for many indoor gatherings.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The United States was one year into the COVID-19 pandemic, with vaccination campaigns ramping up nationally and deaths continuing to decline. The 2020 presidential election aftermath remained contentious, with ongoing litigation and investigations into the January 6 Capitol riot dominating political news. Major League Baseball's 2021 season had just begun (opening day was March 29), with sports gradually returning to near-normal operations.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

India was experiencing a devastating second wave of COVID-19 in early April 2021, with hospitals overwhelmed and death counts rising sharply. The Myanmar military coup (February 1) remained a major story with ongoing protests and civil disobedience against the junta. Israel-Palestine tensions were building toward the May 2021 escalation, though major violence had not yet erupted at this point.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American society in early April 2021 occupied a liminal space: vaccination hope was rising, but pandemic fatigue and uncertainty persisted, with many unsure when "normal" would truly return. The nation remained deeply polarized politically following January 6 and the 2020 election. For many churches, including Episcopal congregations, Good Friday 2021 represented the first major holiday observance with possibility of fuller in-person participation than Easter 2020, making themes of resurrection and hope particularly resonant after a year of loss and isolation.
